title: Live RAM acquisition
action: Capture volatile memory before powering the system down. Use a vetted acquisition tool (Magnet RAM Capture, WinPMEM, DumpIt, or AVML on Linux) writing to an external drive that's separate from the suspect storage. Compute SHA-256 of the resulting raw or AFF4 dump immediately and record it in the chain-of-custody form.
rationale: Running processes, network connections, encryption keys, and decrypted DPAPI material live in RAM and disappear at shutdown. Cases involving credential theft, ransomware execution, or anti-forensics tooling routinely turn on artefacts only memory analysis can recover (BitLocker / VeraCrypt keys, in-memory C2 strings, parent-child process trees the disk forensics never sees).
expected_result: Memory dump file (.raw / .lime / .aff4) written to the external collection drive; size matches installed RAM; SHA-256 computed and logged.

axiom.action: Magnet RAM Capture → select output path on the external drive → Start. RAM Capture writes a .raw file with built-in hash logging. Once complete, ingest the .raw into AXIOM Process as a Memory Image evidence source for downstream analysis.
axiom.ui_path: Magnet RAM Capture (standalone tool that ships with AXIOM)
axiom.notes: RAM Capture is free and runs from a USB stick — no install footprint on the suspect machine.

xways.action: X-Ways doesn't ship a memory acquirer; pair it with an external tool. Run WinPMEM (.exe from the X-Ways bench USB) targeting the external drive: `winpmem.exe -o E:\evidence\mem.raw`. Hash the output, then add the .raw as an evidence object in X-Ways for raw-string scanning.
xways.ui_path: External WinPMEM > X-Ways > Add Evidence Object > File

autopsy.action: Use DumpIt (Comae) or WinPMEM externally; Autopsy's memory ingest is limited but you can register the dump as a logical file evidence source for keyword search and string carving. Pair with Volatility for the deep analysis pass.
autopsy.ui_path: External DumpIt / WinPMEM > Autopsy > Add Data Source > Logical Files
